programme.title: Unbeatable

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz show where being in control is everything. Who will emerge as the Boss and walk away with the cash? Only the strongest players will survive the game.

programme.title: The Vintage French Farmhouse
programme.desc: In Villeneuve-lés-Avignon, James is drawn to a stunning stone sculpture, while Jacqui zones in on a portable French gramophone.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist, Ben Robinson visits the east coast village of Flamborough which sits on a rugged headland sticking out into the North Sea. A largely unassuming village today, it was once effectively cut off from the rest of the country by a five-metre-deep, two-and-a-half-mile long man-made dyke, re-enforced and fortified with local chalk. As Ben discovers when he visits two of the village's most prominent structures, chalk plays a large part in the history of Flamborough.

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: The team venture into one of the world's most hostile environments, the Arctic Ocean, which plays a crucial role in controlling our climate. The team plan to dive beneath the polar ice cap to explore how the ice, the dominant feature of this ocean, is shrinking and how this could affect the rest of the world.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin and the team are on the south-east coast, in the seaside town of Bexhill-on-Sea. But there's no time for sunbathing as experts Michael Baggott and David Fletcher have their work cut out digging and delving through bags and boxes looking for those unwanted antiques. David finds a charming art deco children's clock while Michael may have discovered a painting that could be worth thousands!

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Former players Josh Jones, Sarah Keyworth, Melvin Odoom and Kimberly Wyatt test their general knowledge skills on this week's Richard Osman's House of Games: Redemption Week.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Celebrity players Nitin Ganatra, the Rev Kate Bottley and Alex Gray, aka Apollo from Gladiators, face another knowledge battle, led by Vernon Kay and aided by unreliable tour guide Paul Gorton.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining three chefs from Scotland compete over mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. Who will win, and who must go home?

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Warship
programme.desc: HMS Queen Elizabeth is still under construction at Rosyth Dockyard in Scotland as Captain Jerry Kyd and his 700 sailors prepare for dangerous sea trials. Before sailing, the crew have to undergo rigorous fire and flood training.

programme.title: Hostage
programme.desc: In 2012, photojournalists John Cantlie and James Foley chase a scoop in Syria, but their reporting draws the attention of jihadists, and the net closes in on them.

programme.title: We Might Regret This
programme.desc: Freya and Jo navigate grief, missteps and misunderstandings. Abe's staged proposal sparks confusion and unexpected tensions.

He visits the church and meets Matt Champion, an expert in medieval graffiti who has found some incredible links to past villagers.

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using the latest 3D scanning technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott uncover 2,500 years of hidden history in Naples from its earliest Greek and Roman origins. They explore how the volcano of Mount Vesuvius both nurtured the region and exacted a terrible price on the local population. Plus, they delve into a labyrinth of fascinating underground spaces that helped build and sustain the city.

programme.title: Flog It!

Adam gets in a spin over a wooden top that reminds him of his childhood, and Kate hopes she's struck gold with some coins that could be worth thousands. Paul takes a break from the antiques and tries his hand at some woodwork - meeting a local man who's been making violins for over 40 years.

It was found that over 2,000 people were living here which, at the time, which would have made it the largest settlement anywhere of its kind in Britain.

Featuring Peter Sarstedt's Where Do You Go To My Lovely? a number one in 1969, a hit he never really matched; Trio's 1982 smash Da Da Da; Phyllis Nelson's 1985 lovers rock-style classic Move Closer; and the New Radicals' 1999 hit You Get What You Give.
